title: Bookbot Outcomes, Repository Baseline, and Evidence · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for bookbot outcomes, repository baseline, and evidence and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.
Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.
Inspect Git status, branch, remotes, configuration origin, tracked files, ignore rules, and baseline tests before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Evidence Ladder: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 1308 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate source, transformation, test, command, package, release, and stakeholder evidence so one green signal cannot hide another failure.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate source, transformation, test, command, package, release, and stakeholder evidence so one green signal cannot hide another failure.
